blockingIssues.description: The milestone still shipped two HeroShaderCanvas mounts, so the required single site-wide shader state was not achieved.
ASCII-specific references remained in tests and source comments, so the requested zero-ASCII end state was not fully reached.
Scanline texture layers still shipped in the atmosphere stack, so the approved single-shader-plus-film-grain implementation was not fully met.
The shared glass-card treatment did not provide the required cursor-tracking liquid-metal sheen across migrated cards.
Some content modules, including the FDV ratchet explainer, still used older accent-card styling instead of the new liquid-metal card system.
Electric blue #455eff remained a general UI accent in text, chips, borders, and panels instead of atmospheric gradients only.
Neon green #00ff00 still appeared as filled CTA or badge treatment instead of status-dot-only signaling.
Global body copy still defaulted to white instead of the required #a0a0a0 body color.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/tradebetter-exact-design-reference.md` to state that the approved visual-re-engineering atmosphere uses film grain only and does not ship a separate scanline layer.
Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to remove stale sticky-inspector/minimap guidance and record the current one-lightweight-orientation graph-shell direction.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make the tradebetter extraction reuse path explicit: if a worker reuses committed FetchUrl/pinchtab artifacts instead of rerunning extraction, require the exact artifact path in the handoff or mark `followedProcedure=false`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviews for `redesign-tradebetter-exact-design-tokens-and-typography` and `redesign-single-shader-and-film-grain-atmosphere` both found `followedProcedure=true` even though the saved transcripts only showed reuse of committed tradebetter-reference material and did not include the required extraction-step evidence or explicit reused-artifact citation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify step 14 so deletion/purge and test-only features have an explicit exception path (for example post-removal assertions or coverage-only verification) instead of forcing artificial pre-change failing tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviews for `redesign-purge-ascii-and-duplicate-atmosphere` and `redesign-visual-016-ascii-removed-contract-update` both reported that the current test-first rule is too rigid for deletion-only or coverage-only features and caused otherwise-correct workers to mark procedure deviations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align shell guidance with the current visual-re-engineering direction by removing sticky-inspector/minimap defaults and requiring one lightweight orientation model for graph-shell work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `redesign-clean-graph-workspace-and-information-flow` cites `frontend-experience-worker` step 12 recommending minimap and sticky-inspector patterns even though the current mission guidance explicitly removed that chrome.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten procedure-audit rules so `followedProcedure=true` requires transcript-visible evidence of required library/reference reads and a cited pre-implementation failing expectation when step 14 applies.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews in this round (`redesign-monochrome-badges-and-status-dots`, `redesign-clean-graph-workspace-and-information-flow`, and `redesign-tradebetter-exact-design-tokens-and-typography`) found workers reporting `followedProcedure=true` even when the transcript did not show the required design-system/reference reads or the handoff omitted the specific failing expectation cited by the skill.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile liquid-metal card blur guidance across mission artifacts so features, AGENTS guidance, and library references all say the same thing about whether backdrop blur is allowed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `redesign-liquid-metal-cards-and-glass-morphism` review found the feature contract requiring `backdrop-filter: blur(10px)` while mission AGENTS and architecture guidance described the approved liquid-metal cards as opacity-only glass with no backdrop blur.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated scanline-vs-film-grain mismatch reports from both the ASCII-purge and single-shader reviews.
Repeated procedure-audit notes about missing transcript-visible design-system/reference reads despite `followedProcedure=true`.
The lack of a documented home for ad hoc visual audit artifacts such as `VISUAL_UX_AUDIT.md`.
